title: Oncidium reichenheimii
intro: Oncidium reichenheimii, commonly known as the 'Reichenheim's Oncidium', is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Oncidium reichenheimii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oncidium reichenheimii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the production of cough syrups and expectorants.
taxonomy: Oncidium reichenheimii belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. It is a part of the subfamily Cymbidieae and tribe Maxillarieae.
